content.full_text:
FFPC
AUG 5 1990

LYNN A. SCHENK
ATTORNEY AT LAW
101 WEST BROADWAY
SUITE 1500
SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA 92101
(619) 231-8700

July 30, 1990

Mr. John Wallace
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814


Dear John:

     The following is a summary of my consulting agreement
with the law firm of Lorenz, Alhadeff, Lundin & Oggel,
dated April 15, 1988

--the association between myself and the firm can be
terminated with thirty days written notice;

--my professional services are available to clients
referred to me by the firm as well as those who bring to
the firm.  My legal services are offered exclusively
through the firm, but I am free to do other consulting
outside the firm;

--my compensation is based on a percentage as follows:

     (a) where I bring a client to the firm and legal
services are performed by other attorneys in the firm, I am
entitled to a percentage of the professional fees billed
and collected from the client; the balance belongs to the
firm;

     (b) where I perform services for a client I have
brought to the firm, the firm is entitled to a certain
percentage of the total professional fees billed and
collected;

     (c) where the firm refers a client to me, I receive
a percentage of the total fees billed and collected for my
services and the firm retains the remainder;

--Paragraph seven of the agreement states as follows:

"Counsel and the firm both agree that counsel's
association with the firm shall be deemed to create an
independent contractual relationship, and that counsel
shall not be an employee or partner of the firm for any
purpose, including income tax withholding, workers
compensation, unemployment benefits or the like. Counsel
shall not be liable for the firm's obligations or
liabilities, and the firm agrees to indemnify counsel and
hold counsel harmless against any such liability unless it
arises from or is caused by the act or omission of
counsel."

--As I have said in previous conversations and
correspondence, I have no ownership or other interest in
the firm or any of its clients for which I do no work.

If you or any of your colleagues have questions,
please feel free to give me a call. Again, thank you for
your assistance in this matter.

Sincerely,

Lynn A. Schenk

LAS/jle

LYNN A. SCHENK
ATTORNEY AT LAW
101 WEST BROADWAY
SUITE 1500
SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA 92101
(619) 231-8700

July 5, 1990


Mr. John Wallace, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814


Dear Mr. Wallace:

     Per our conversation, enclosed is my disclosure
statement.  My spouse, C. Hugh Friedman looks forward to
seeing you on July 19, at about 1:00 p.m.  Thanks again!

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Lynn A. Schenk


LAS/jle
sche0248.ltr

Enc.: Disclosure Statement

LORENZ ALHADEFF LUNDIN & OGGEL


TO:     John Wallace, Esq.                    DATE:    July 9, 1990
        Fair Political Practices Commission
                                        FILE NO:   1017.001

FROM:   Lynn Schenk                     COPIES TO:
        San Diego Port Commissioner
SUBJECT: FPPC Regulations


        As we have discussed in our two telephone conversations, I have
several questions regarding the conflict of interest regulations which
apply to my appointed position as a San Diego Port Commissioner. At
your suggestion, I am putting the questions in writing for further
discussion.

        I have also provided you with a copy of my "Assuming Office
Disclosure Statement" and my consulting arrangement with the law firm
of Lorenz Alhadeff Lundin & Oggel (LALO). Again to recap, I have a
sole proprietorship in a legal and consulting practice and a San
Diego business license. The law practice side of my business is done
through a consulting arrangement with the above-referenced law firm.

        With respect to disclosure requirements, it is my understanding
that on "Schedule D" the source of income which needs to be disclosed
is my law practice, my spouse's law practice, and my spouse's salary as
a law school professor. Further, specific sources of income must be
disclosed on "Schedule H-2", if they meet the threshold disclosure
requirements of being a source of income in excess of $10,000 and doing
business in the jurisdiction (e.g. the LALO law firm and other clients
meeting the threshold requirements). I would appreciate confirmation
of this understanding.

        With regard to conflict of interest, I understand that I must
abstain from participating in a vote which would affect an applicant
who is also a source of income to my spouse or me. My questions are as
follows:

        1.) What is my obligation when an applicant is known by me to
            be a client of LALO but is not a source of income to my spouse
            or me. That is, I have not derived any income from that
            particular client and only know it to be a client of the law
            firm?

        2.) What is my responsibility regarding an applicant as
            described in #1 above who is represented before the Port
            Commission by a lawyer from LALO?

        3.) What is my obligation to ascertain whether an applicant in
            whom I have no financial interest is a client of LALO?

MEMORANDUM TO John Wallace, Esq.
July 9, 1990
Page 2
_________________________


     4.)  What if an applicant is a client of LALO unbeknown to me?

     5.)  What is my responsibility to know who my spouse's clients
are?  What if one appears before the Port, but I am unaware that
he/she is a client of my spouse? What if a client of my spouse
has a matter before the Port unknown to my spouse or me?

     6.)  What is my responsibility when a matter before the Port may
have a direct or indirect impact upon a client from whom my
spouse or I have received income but I am unaware that there
would be such an impact?

          a.)  What is my responsibility in ascertaining potential
     direct or indirect impact on clients in every action coming
     before the Board of Port Commissioners?

               Hypothetical: assume the City of National City has
          come before the Board of Port Commissioners for a zoning
          change of its property within the Port District, assume
          further that client "X", not located in the City of
          National City, has appeared to support or oppose the
          request, what is my responsibility in voting on the
          request?  Now assume that client "X" did not make an
          appearance but is affected by the decision, what is my
          obligation to know of such an effect?




sche0250.mem/3
1017.001

LORENZ ALHADEFF LUNDIN & OGGEL

        Fair Political Practices Commission
                                                    FILE NO:  1017.001
FROM:   Lynn Schenk                                 COPIES TO:
        San Diego Port Commissioner
SUBJECT: FPPC Regulations


As we have discussed in our two telephone conversations, I have
several questions regarding the conflict of interest regulations which
apply to my appointed position as a San Diego Port Commissioner. At
your suggestion, I am putting the questions in writing for further
discussion.

     I have also provided you with a copy of my "Assuming Office
Disclosure Statement" and my consulting arrangement with the law firm
of Lorenz Alhadeff Lundin & Oggel (LALO).  Again to recap, I have a
sole proprietorship in a legal and consulting practice and have a San
Diego business license.  The law practice side of my business is done
through a consulting arrangement with the above-referenced law firm.

     With respect to disclosure requirements, it is my understanding
that on "Schedule D" the source of income which needs to be disclosed
is my law practice, my spouse's law practice, and my spouse's salary as
a high school professor.  Further, specific sources of income must be
disclosed on "Schedule H-2", if they meet the threshold disclosure
requirements of being a source of income in excess of $10,000 and doing
business in the jurisdiction (e.g. the LALO law firm and other clients
meeting the threshold requirements).  I would appreciate confirmation
of this understanding.

     With regard to conflict of interest, I understand that I must
abstain from participating in a vote which would affect an applicant
who is also a source of income to my spouse or me.  My questions are as
follows:

     1.) What is my obligation when an applicant is known by me to
be a client of LALO but is not a source of income to my spouse
or me.  That is, I have not derived any income from that
particular client and only know it to be a client of the law
firm?

     2.) What is my responsibility regarding an applicant as
described in #1 above who is represented before the Port
Commission by a lawyer from LALO?

     3.) What is my obligation to ascertain whether an applicant in
whom I have no financial interest is a client of LALO?

MEMORANDUM TO John Wallace, Esq.
July 9, 1990
Page 2
_________________________


     4.)  What if an applicant is a client of LALO unbeknown to me?

     5.)  What is my responsibility to know who my spouse's clients
are?  What if one appears before the Port, but I am unaware that
he/she is a client of my spouse?  What if a client of my spouse
has a matter before the Port unknown to my spouse or me?

     6.)  What is my responsibility when a matter before the Port may
have a direct or indirect impact upon a client from whom my
spouse or I have received income but I am unaware that there
would be such an impact?

          a.)  What is my responsibility in ascertaining potential
direct or indirect impact on clients in every action coming
before the Board of Port Commissioners?

               Hypothetical: assume the City of National City has
          come before the Board of Port Commissioners for a zoning
          change of its property within the Port District, assume
          further that client "X", not located in the City of
          National City, has appeared to support or oppose the
          request, what is my responsibility in voting on the
          request?  Now assume that client "X" did not make an
          appearance but is affected by the decision, what is my
          obligation to know of such an effect?

sections.question:
1.) What is my obligation when an applicant is known by me to be a client of LALO but is not a source of income to my spouse or me. That is, I have not derived any income from that particular client and only know it to be a client of the law firm?

2.) What is my responsibility regarding an applicant as described in #1 above who is represented before the Port Commission by a lawyer from LALO?

3.) What is my obligation to ascertain whether an applicant in whom I have no financial interest is a client of LALO?

4.) What if an applicant is a client of LALO unbeknown to me?

5.) What is my responsibility to know who my spouse's clients are? What if one appears before the Port, but I am unaware that he/she is a client of my spouse? What if a client of my spouse has a matter before the Port unknown to my spouse or me?

6.) What is my responsibility when a matter before the Port may have a direct or indirect impact upon a client from whom my spouse or I have received income but I am unaware that there would be such an impact?

7.) What is my responsibility in ascertaining potential direct or indirect impact on clients in every action coming before the Board of Port Commissioners? [Includes hypothetical regarding City of National City zoning change]

sections.question_synthetic: Lynn Schenk seeks clarification on her conflict of interest obligations as a San Diego Port Commissioner, specifically regarding: (1) disclosure requirements for sources of income from her law practice and spouse's income; (2) whether she must abstain from votes affecting LALO clients even when she has not personally derived income from them; and (3) the extent of her duty to ascertain whether applicants are clients of LALO or her spouse's practice.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: This is an incoming request/inquiry document, not an FPPC response letter. The document contains multiple copies/iterations of the memorandum with identical content. No formal FPPC conclusion or advice section is present. Some OCR quality issues in source material but key question sections are clearly legible. Requestor references previous telephone conversations with John Wallace at FPPC.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: 1.) What is my obligation when an applicant is known by me to be a client of LALO but is not a source of income to my spouse or me. That is, I have not derived any income from that particular client and only know it to be a client of the law firm?

2.) What is my responsibility regarding an applicant as described in #1 above who is represented before the Port Commission by a lawyer from LALO?

3.) What is my obligation to ascertain whether an applicant in whom I have no financial interest is a client of LALO?

4.) What if an applicant is a client of LALO unbeknown to me?

5.) What is my responsibility to know who my spouse's clients are? What if one appears before the Port, but I am unaware that he/she is a client of my spouse? What if a client of my spouse has a matter before the Port unknown to my spouse or me?

6.) What is my responsibility when a matter before the Port may have a direct or indirect impact upon a client from whom my spouse or I have received income but I am unaware that there would be such an impact?

7.) What is my responsibility in ascertaining potential direct or indirect impact on clients in every action coming before the Board of Port Commissioners? [Includes hypothetical regarding City of National City zoning change]

My legal services are offered exclusively through the firm, but I am free to do other consulting outside the firm; --my compensation is based on a percentage as follows: (a) where I bring a client to the firm and legal services are performed by other attorneys in the firm, I am entitled to a percentage of the professional fees billed and collected from the client; the balance belongs to the firm; (b) where I perform services for a client I have brought to the firm, the firm is entitled to a certain percentage of the total professional fees billed and collected; (c) where the firm refers a client to me, I receive a percentage of the total fees billed and collected for my services and the firm retains the remainder; --Paragraph seven of the agreement states as follows: "Counsel and the firm both agree that counsel's association with the firm shall be deemed to create an independent contractual relationship, and that counsel shall not be an employee or partner of the firm for any purpose, including income tax withholding, workers compensation, unemployment benefits or the like. embedding.summary: Lynn Schenk, a San Diego Port Commissioner with a consulting arrangement with law firm LALO, submits a detailed written request to FPPC staff seeking guidance on conflict of interest regulations and disclosure requirements applicable to her appointed position, including multiple specific scenarios regarding applicants who may be clients of the law firm.
